title: Stelis pumila
taxonomy: Stelis pumila is a species of orchid in the genus Stelis. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is classified as a type of terrestrial orchid. The genus Stelis is further classified under the subfamily Epidendroideae, and the family Orchidaceae is a part of the order Asparagales. The order Asparagales is a subgroup of the class Liliopsida, and the class Liliopsida is part of the phylum Magnoliophyta. This classification makes Stelis pumila a member of the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Stelis pumila is a plant species that is often used in traditional medicine, particularly in South America. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial in treating various health conditions. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi, making it a promising candidate for the development of new antimicrobial agents. Additionally, Stelis pumila has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential applications of this plant species.

intro: Stelis pumila is a low-growing, terrestrial orchid species native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its small, delicate flowers and compact growth habit. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Stelis pumila include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other phytochemicals, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Stelis pumila is often prepared as a tincture or infused in tea, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create formulas for treating coughs, sore throats, and digestive issues.
